[[File:Relevance Realization.svg|thumb]]
Religio is the entire right hand side of this [[Relevance realization]] diagram<ref>https://www.meaningcrisis.co/ep-33-awakening-from-the-meaning-crisis-the-spirituality-of-rr-wonder-awe-mystery-sacredness/</ref><ref>[[Reacting to the Meaning Crisis]] episode 33</ref>
==Google doc quote==
This term refers to the phenomenology of relevance-realization in terms of meaning-making. Religio is “the sense of a pre-egoic and ultimately post-egoic binding that simultaneously grounds the self and its world.” It is the generalized referencereferene of something like religion.  This overlaps with the kind of meaning-making that has been gathered together in many discourses in the term “spirituality”.<ref name=":0">https://docs.google.com/document/d/1FWZSdPa996VseFAJFfQ-Xu4RN_2JoK9V7maoFmzXgaE/edit#</ref>

Salient features/aspects of religio that relevance-realization can account for:<ref name=":0" />

* The possibility of complexification, of self-transcendence<ref name=":0" />
* The possibility of self-deception, bullshitting<ref name=":0" />
* Connectedness<ref name=":0" />
* Perspectival knowing<ref name=":0" />
* Participatory knowing<ref name=":0" />
* Caring, significance<ref name=":0" />
* The cognitive continuum from fluency, to insight, to flow, to altered states of consciousness, to higher states of consciousness.<ref name=":0" />
* Fundamental framing<ref name=":0" />

The term religio is being used deliberately. It recalls the word “religion” and its connotations, but its meaning is not identical to the word religion. Religio is one of the purported etymological origins of the word “religio”, coming from the latin “Religare”, meaning literally “to read back”, or “to bind together”, “reconnect”.<ref name=":0" />

Relevant episode: 33<ref name=":0" />

== See Also ==
[[Relevance realization]]<ref name=":0" />

==References==

[[Category:John Vervaeke]]
[[sn:Religio]][[Category:Sapient Psychology]]
[[Category:Git synced pages]]
[[sn:Religio]][[sn:Religio]][[Category:Knowledge]]
[[sn:Religio]]
[[Category:Pages]]
